unit.name: Early multimodal modeling patterns
unit.description: Surveys pre-transformer deep multimodal ideas built around shared embedding spaces, grounding, captioning, attention, and text-conditioned generation.
unit.summary: The early recipe is to encode vision and language separately and align them in a shared embedding space with a similarity score and margin-based objective so similar image-text items move together and dissimilar ones move apart [ts=476s][ts=491s]. Kiela then walks through bag-of-visual-words style grounding, CNN-plus-word-embedding multimodal word vectors, and skip-gram-style prediction of image features from words, arguing that grounding gives richer semantic representations [ts=598s][ts=680s]. He extends the story from words to sentences, where LSTM or recursive sentence encoders are aligned with images, and notes that sentence encoders trained to predict pictures transfer well to downstream NLP tasks [ts=720s][ts=780s]. The segment ends with encoder-decoder captioning, visual attention that aligns generated words to image regions, and text-conditioned GAN image generation as the early path toward modern text-to-image systems [ts=813s][ts=873s][ts=904s].

key_points.text: A basic early multimodal model aligns vision and language encoders in one embedding space using a similarity score and margin loss.
Bag of visual words uses SIFT keypoints, local descriptors, clustering, and counts to form a visual analogue of bag of words.
Predicting image features for words or sentences can yield richer grounded semantic representations.
Image captioning emerges by replacing the source-language encoder in seq2seq with a ConvNet image encoder.
Attention lets a captioning model align generated words like “stop” to the relevant image region such as a stop sign.
GANs enable text-conditioned image generation by training a generator to fool a discriminator while conditioning on text prompts.

kp_catalog.name: Cross-modal alignment and grounding
kp_catalog.description: The idea that language and perception can be aligned in shared spaces or through predictive grounding objectives to improve semantic representations.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because cross-modal alignment and grounding formalizes how the idea that language and perception can be aligned in shared spaces or through predictive grounding objectives to improve semantic representations, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.
